Package org.key_project.util.collection

Interface ImmutableList<T>

All Superinterfaces:
Iterable<T>, Serializable
All Known Implementing Classes:
ImmutableSLList
public interface ImmutableList<T> extends Iterable<T>, Serializable
List interface to be implemented by non-destructive lists

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    ImmutableList<T>
    append(Iterable<T> collection)
    appends an iterable collection
    ImmutableList<T>
    append(ImmutableList<T> list)
    appends a whole list (non-destructive)
    ImmutableList<T>
    append(T element)
    appends element to the list (non-destructive)
    ImmutableList<T>
    append(T... array)
    appends element at end (non-destructive) (O(n))

    Returns a Collector that accumulates the input elements into a new ImmutableList.
    boolean

    boolean
    exists(Predicate<T> predicate)
    return true if predicate is fullfilled for at least one element
    default ImmutableList<T>

    Returns an immutable list consisting of the elements of this list that match the given predicate.

    Creates an ImmutableList from a List.

    Get the last element of this list.

    Returns an immutable list consisting of the results of applying the given function to the elements of this list.

    Return an empty immutable list.
    static <T> ImmutableList<T>

    Return a singleton immutable list.

    Return an immutable list with the iterated elements.

    Return an immutable list with two elements.

    of(T e1, T e2, T e3)
    Return an immutable list with three elements.
    ImmutableList<T>
    prepend(ImmutableList<T> list)
    prepends a whole list (non-destructive)
    ImmutableList<T>
    prepend(T element)
    prepends element to the list (non-destructive)
    ImmutableList<T>
    prepend(T... array)
    prepends array (O(n))
    ImmutableList<T>
    prependReverse(Iterable<T> collection)
    prepends an iterable collection in reverse order, i.e., [4,5,6].prepend([1,2,3]) will be [3,2,1,4,5,6]
    ImmutableList<T>
    prependReverse(ImmutableList<T> collection)
    prepends an immutable list in reverse order, i.e., [4,5,6].prepend([1,2,3]) will be [3,2,1,4,5,6] (more efficient than prepend(ImmutableList))
    ImmutableList<T>
    removeAll(T obj)
    removes all occurrences of obj
    ImmutableList<T>
    removeFirst(T obj)
    removes first occurrence of obj

  • Method Details

    • collector

      static <T> Collector<T,List<T>,ImmutableList<T>> collector()
      Returns a Collector that accumulates the input elements into a new ImmutableList.
      Returns:
      a Collector that accumulates the input elements into a new ImmutableList.

    • fromList

      static <T> ImmutableList<T> fromList(Collection<T> list)
      Creates an ImmutableList from a List.
      Parameters:
      list - a List.
      Returns:
      an ImmutableList containing the same elements as the specified list.

    • of

      static <T> ImmutableList<T> of()
      Return an empty immutable list.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the entry type of the list.
      Returns:
      empty immutable list.

    • of

      static <T> ImmutableList<T> of(T e1)
      Return a singleton immutable list.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the entry type of the list.
      Parameters:
      e1 - the element to put into the list
      Returns:
      singleton immutable list.

    • of

      static <T> ImmutableList<T> of(T e1, T e2)
      Return an immutable list with two elements. The iteration order is: e1 then e2
      Type Parameters:
      T - the entry type of the list.
      Parameters:
      e1 - the element to put into the list
      e2 - the element to put into the list
      Returns:
      (e1, e2) as immutable list

    • of

      static <T> ImmutableList<T> of(T e1, T e2, T e3)
      Return an immutable list with three elements. The iteration order is: e1 then e2 then e3
      Type Parameters:
      T - the entry type of the list.
      Parameters:
      e1 - the element to put into the list
      e2 - the element to put into the list
      e3 - the element to put into the list
      Returns:
      (e1, e2, e3) as immutable list

    • of

      static <T> ImmutableList<T> of(T... es)
      Return an immutable list with the iterated elements. The iteration order is the order of the arguments
      Type Parameters:
      T - the entry type of the list.
      Parameters:
      es - the elements to put into the list
      Returns:
      (e1, e2, e3, ...) as immutable list

    • prepend

      ImmutableList<T> prepend(T element)
      prepends element to the list (non-destructive)
      Parameters:
      element - the head of the created list
      Returns:
      IList with the new element as head and this list as tail

    • prepend

      ImmutableList<T> prepend(ImmutableList<T> list)
      prepends a whole list (non-destructive)
      Parameters:
      list - the list to be prepended
      Returns:
      IList list++this

    • prependReverse

      ImmutableList<T> prependReverse(ImmutableList<T> collection)
      prepends an immutable list in reverse order, i.e., [4,5,6].prepend([1,2,3]) will be [3,2,1,4,5,6] (more efficient than prepend(ImmutableList))
      Returns:
      reverse(collection)++this

    • prependReverse

      ImmutableList<T> prependReverse(Iterable<T> collection)
      prepends an iterable collection in reverse order, i.e., [4,5,6].prepend([1,2,3]) will be [3,2,1,4,5,6]
      Returns:
      reverse(collection)++this

    • prepend

      ImmutableList<T> prepend(T... array)
      prepends array (O(n))
      Parameters:
      array - the array of the elements to be prepended
      Returns:
      IList the new list

    • append

      ImmutableList<T> append(T element)
      appends element to the list (non-destructive)
      Parameters:
      element - to be added at the end
      Returns:
      IList with the new element at the end

    • append

      ImmutableList<T> append(ImmutableList<T> list)
      appends a whole list (non-destructive)
      Parameters:
      list - the list to be appended
      Returns:
      IList this++list

    • append

      ImmutableList<T> append(Iterable<T> collection)
      appends an iterable collection

    • append

      ImmutableList<T> append(T... array)
      appends element at end (non-destructive) (O(n))
      Parameters:
      array - the array to be appended
      Returns:
      IList the new list

    • head

      T head()
      Returns:
      the first element in list

    • exists

      boolean exists(Predicate<T> predicate)
      return true if predicate is fullfilled for at least one element
      Parameters:
      predicate - the predicate
      Returns:
      true if predicate is fullfilled for at least one element

    • tail

      ImmutableList<T> tail()
      Returns:
      IList tail of list

    • take

      ImmutableList<T> take(int n)
      Returns:
      IList this list without the first n elements

    • reverse

      ImmutableList<T> reverse()
      Reverses this list

    • iterator

      Iterator<T> iterator()
      Specified by:
      iterator in interface Iterable<T>
      Returns:
      Iterator of this list

    • contains

      boolean contains(T obj)
      Returns:
      boolean is true iff. obj is in List

    • size

      int size()
      Returns:
      int representing number of elements in list

    • isEmpty

      boolean isEmpty()
      Returns:
      true iff the list is empty

    • removeFirst

      ImmutableList<T> removeFirst(T obj)
      removes first occurrence of obj
      Returns:
      new list

    • removeAll

      ImmutableList<T> removeAll(T obj)
      removes all occurrences of obj
      Returns:
      new list

    • toArray

      <S> S[] toArray(S[] array)
      Convert the list to a Java array (O(n))

    • toArray

      <S> S[] toArray(Class<S> type)
      Convert the list to a Java array (O(n))

    • stream

      default Stream<T> stream()
      A stream object for this collection.
      Returns:
      a non-null stream object

    • toList

      default List<T> toList()
      Convert an ImmutableList to a List.
      Returns:
      This element converted to a List.

    • filter

      @Nonnull default ImmutableList<T> filter(@Nonnull Predicate<T> predicate)
      Returns an immutable list consisting of the elements of this list that match the given predicate.
      Parameters:
      predicate - a non-interfering, stateless predicate to apply to each element to determine if it should be included

    • map

      default <R> ImmutableList<R> map(Function<T,R> function)
      Returns an immutable list consisting of the results of applying the given function to the elements of this list.
      Type Parameters:
      R - The element type of the result list
      Parameters:
      function - a non-interfering, stateless function to apply to each element
      Returns:
      the mapped list of the same length as this

    • hasPrefix

      default boolean hasPrefix(ImmutableList<T> other)
      Parameters:
      other - prefix to check for
      Returns:
      whether this list starts with the elements of the provided prefix

    • stripPrefix

      default ImmutableList<T> stripPrefix(ImmutableList<T> prefix)
      Remove a prefix from this list.
      Parameters:
      prefix - prefix to remove
      Returns:
      new list with the prefix removed
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the provided prefix is not a prefix of this list

    • last

      default T last()
      Get the last element of this list. Time complexity: O(n).
      Returns:
      last element of this list

    • get

      default T get(int idx)
      Get the n-th element of this list.
      Parameters:
      idx - the 0-based index of the element
      Returns:
      the element at index idx.
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if idx is less than 0 or at least size().
